meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to perform idea validation and overcome a lack of portfolio. A Beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your SaaS.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for a portfolio to start building. Validate your SaaS idea by shipping a micro-MVP in 24 hours. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate on user feedback. Real revenue and active users are the only portfolio items that matter to investors and customers alike.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new repository is only intimidating if you treat it as a final product. Most founders stall because they obsess over the 'portfolio'—a collection of vanity projects that don't solve real-world pain. The truth is, your portfolio isn't a museum of code; it's a track record of solved problems.

We are shifting the paradigm from 'coding for show' to 'building for value.' By leveraging AI-first development tools, you will bypass the months of manual setup and launch a validated micro-SaaS that proves your competency through traction, not just lines of code.

steps.title: Define the High-Pain Niche
steps.description: Identify a specific workflow bottleneck in a B2B niche. Focus on 'hair-on-fire' problems where users are currently using manual spreadsheets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a list of 5 high-pain, manual workflow problems in the legal-tech or accounting space that can be solved with a micro-SaaS. For each, define the MVP feature set and a potential monetization model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research current manual bottlenecks in small law firm document management and propose a micro-SaaS solution'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use AI to define your database schema and API structure, ensuring your foundation is scalable even for a small project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, scalable PostgreSQL schema and a Next.js API structure for a SaaS that manages [Idea Name]. Include authentication and a basic dashboard endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project with Prisma and NextAuth based on the architecture generated by Antigravity'

steps.title: Build the Core Value Loop
steps.description: Don't build features; build the 'aha' moment. Focus exclusively on the feature that solves the primary pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core logic for [Primary Feature]. Ensure it handles edge cases and returns a clear, actionable result for the user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core logic for the document parsing engine and connect it to the database'

steps.title: Implement Rapid Feedback Loops
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment and get it in front of 5 potential users immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a feedback form and a post-submission webhook that logs user data to a Google Sheet for manual analysis.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a feedback widget to the dashboard and connect it to a webhook for real-time notifications'

steps.title: Launch and Iterate
steps.description: Ship the product and use the metrics to refine the code. Your portfolio is now a live, revenue-generating SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the logs from my user feedback and suggest 3 high-impact features to improve retention for the next sprint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current logs and refactor the dashboard UI based on user feedback trends'

pro_tips: Never build a landing page before you have a functional core feature; code the solution first, then wrap it in marketing.
Use 'Shadow Deployments' to test features with a subset of users before committing to a full production release.
Focus on 'Data Moats'—design your app so that the longer a user stays, the more valuable their data becomes.
If you aren't embarrassed by the first version of your product, you shipped too late.
Treat every bug report as a direct request for a new feature; prioritize those that prevent churn.
resources: Indie Hackers SaaS Validation Guide: https://indiehackers.com
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on cold outreach to 50 prospects in your niche. Use their feedback to refine your product, then document the entire journey as a 'Build in Public' case study.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building toy projects and start shipping real software that generates revenue and builds your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
